## TCC GENERAL SDK (Google auto-traslate)

[![bnpm](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/badge/v/@byted-service/tcc-general.svg?style=flat-square)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/tcc-general) [![download](https://2y8ngapf.fn.bytedance.net/d?p=@byted-service/tcc-general)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/tcc-general)

[简体中文](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/tcc-general/README.md) | [English](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/tcc-general/README.en.md) | [CHANGELOG](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/tcc-general/CHANGELOG.md)

> [TCC general docs](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnjcN6Uy6YG7Ohl5JuQOEmsd)

> [SDK design](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnyDHs1ZI7N1d0f3Ch3WAycM)

### background

The current design of TCC is too biased towards the dynamic configuration of business microservices, but when facing the configuration requirements of some basic components or large-scale platforms, TCC support is more laborious, such as:

1. [AIOps Configuration Center Requirements](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnTvqy9U4HBiGwz27FIJKU2d)
2. The configuration requirements of components such as open-tracing/bdiff/eventbus

The main shortcomings of TCC:

1. For the demand for the number of keys, currently TCC is limited to 1000, but the keys that need to be configured for component classes are generally linked to PSM, and the magnitude will be very large
2. For the requirements of the key organization structure, the business needs to have an associated hierarchical structure. At present, the TCC is an independent flat structure, which is not convenient for group management
3. Limitation of value size, currently limited by TCC design, the total value size of a service is limited
4. For configuration inheritance/override requirements, support common configuration and characteristic configuration

Therefore, a general configuration center is needed that can support this generalized requirement and cover the configuration requirements of other non-microservices

### installation

```bash
npm install @byted-service/tcc-general --registry=http://bnpm.byted.org
```

### config options

```ts
interface Options {
    // serviceName
    serviceName: string;
    // cluster, default is `default`
    cluster?: string;
    // cache time, unit is ms , minimum is 3000ms, default is 6000ms
    cacheTime?: number;
    // timeout for get config from remote when cache is expired.
    getTimeout?: number;
    // disable print log，default is true
    disableLog?: boolean;
}
```

### quick start

```ts
import TccGeneral from '@byted-service/tcc-general';

(async function test() {
    const tccGeneral = TccGeneral.create({
        serviceName: '<serviceName>',
    });

    let res;
    try {
        // normal config node, watch out the exception that throw by sdk
        res = await tccGeneral.get('<nodeName>');
        console.log('getNode res: ', res);
    } catch (error) {
        if (error.code === TccErrorCode.errKeyNotFound) {
            console.log('[tcc-general] key not found, error: ', error);
        } else {
            console.log('[tcc-general] get error : ', error);
        }
    }

    try {
        // group config node
        res = await tccGeneral.getGroupKey('<nodeName>', '<key>');
        console.log('getGroupKey res: ', res);
    } catch (error) {
        if (error.code === TccErrorCode.errKeyNotFound) {
            console.log('[tcc-general] key not found, error: ', error);
        } else {
            console.log('[tcc-general] getGroupKey error : ', error);
        }
    }
})().then(() => {});
```
